channel.title: Photo Journal on MarkJacobsen.net
channel.description: Recent content in Photo Journal on MarkJacobsen.net

item.title: Early morning beach walk
item.description: [1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/IMG_2754-scaled.jpg?ssl=1

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/26/2015
item.description: It’s an interesting and exciting time to be getting into real estate. Shouldn’t you have one of the most tech savey agents in the area on your side?

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/17/2015
item.description:
﻿

Had a great time outside today. Nice to be on the swings again even if a hat and mittens were necessary. ﻿

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/14/2015
item.description:
﻿

The thing I like most when working with buyers is to find a home that doesn’t look like much from the outside, but when you walk in something like this beautiful sight hits you. Love the natural light. Love the floor to ceiling ﻿windows.

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/8/2015
item.description: Me and the infamous puke bucket got very familiar today. Can’t remember the last time I felt this lousy (don’t think I have at least in my adult life)

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/7/2015
item.description:
﻿

Always good to see good friends and support their endeavors. ﻿

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/6/2015
item.description:
﻿

Waiting around for a baby to be born and having daddy try to get some work done sometimes means allowing this tornado. 

Don’t worry dear, it got cleaned up :)﻿

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/4/2015
item.description:
﻿﻿

Someone lost another tooth… And is it just me or does that look gross?

item.title: Photo Journal for 3/1/2015
item.description:
﻿﻿

A little early birthday celebration for Grandpa.

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/22/2015
item.description: Two days. Two trains. Still was good to see my Grandpa and some family in a whirlwind journey to WI.

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/21/2015
item.description: Hoping it’s not the last time, but in all likelihood it is.

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/19/2015
item.description:
Puttin’ on the Ritz… Cause we’re fancy like that in our jeans 🙂

“The key is to act like you know what you’re doing”

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/18/2015
item.description: Some days it never ceases to amaze me how small the world is, and how fortunate we are to have so many great friends. Thanks today to the Stuber family for having us over to enjoy the beach and pool!

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/17/2015
item.description: Explain to me why I would come back

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/16/2014
item.description: Where’s my driver? This walking thing is for the birds.

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/15/2015
item.description: Let’s blow this Popsicle stand

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/14/2015
item.description:
[][1]
There was a slight wintery mix this morning, and by slight I mean pretty much a white out.[1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1859.jpg

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/13/2015
item.description:
[][1]
Another fun time at the Goldfish Swim School family swim. Always good to see so many friendly faces.[1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1857.jpg

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/11/2015
item.description:
[][1]
The photo doesn’t do it justice so hop on over to [FreedomRE.com][2] and check it out.

Just a few of the recent enhancements include the new search functionality (MLS or address), the ability to deliver saved searches right to your inbox, the ability to save homes to your “favorites”, and a bunch more that will help me serve you better![1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1854.jpg [2]: http://freedomre.com

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/9/2015
item.description:
[][1]
HOMR got a flashy new grill 🙂[1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1849.jpg

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/8/2015
item.description:
[][1]
Had a great morning building snow forts and a snowman with the girls. Once again realized how fortunate we are when our neighbor brought over the snowman kit shown here so we didn’t have to use the jack-o-lantern kit we were using. 🙂[1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1846.jpg

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/7/2015
item.description:
[][1]
Fortunate to have so many great friends. I was able to take Ali to the Spartan game today and we even got on the jumbo tron after posting this selfie to Twitter! Check it out at http://twitter.com/EastLansingRE[1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1826.jpg

item.title: Photo Journal for 2/6/2015
item.description:
[][1]
Very cool midnight cross country skiing w good friends.[1]: https://i0.wp.com/markjacobsen.net/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/IMG_1813.jpg

item.title: Photo Journal for February 05, 2015 at 09:15PM
item.description: Hey, it’s not a picture about snow! Did have the opportunity to meet with a valued partner with Churchill Mortgage today!

item.title: Photo Journal for February 04, 2015 at 08:18PM
item.description: Good thing I have a handy, strong, and resourceful wife 🙂

item.title: Photo Journal for February 03, 2015 at 06:28PM
item.description: Thank you plow, garbage truck, or mailman.

item.title: Photo Journal for February 02, 2015 at 08:20PM
item.description: Are you tired of seeing snow pictures yet?

item.title: Photo Journal for February 01, 2015 at 10:31PM
item.description: Still going….

item.title: Photo Journal for February 01, 2015 at 02:27AM
item.description: It’s begun

item.title: Photo Journal for January 25, 2015 at 11:26PM
item.description: Puzzle pallozza

item.title: Photo Journal for January 29, 2015 at 09:21PM
item.description: Absolutely amazed at these 2. No school because of ice and they played the entire day without any major blow ups. Because of it I actually had a productive day working from home. Amazing when the stars align.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 23, 2015 at 08:44PM
item.description: This year’s read-a-thon selection

item.title: Photo Journal for January 28, 2015 at 07:31AM
item.description: PSA: keep your plastic water bottles off the bottom rack of the dish washer unless you want to generate enough heat when it melts to start melting the other plastic things

item.title: Photo Journal for January 24, 2015 at 11:17PM
item.description: Another good night with good friends.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 27, 2015 at 08:08PM
item.description: Reminds me of all the time I spent building things as a kid. Sure would have preferred to have been playing Legos today vs work.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 22, 2015 at 11:08PM
Photo Journal for January 21, 2015 at 07:19PM
item.description: Don’t forget to drink lots of liquids to help your organs! Recommended: 1) don’t touch infected pigs. 2) get lots of rest. 3) drink lots of liquids

item.title: Photo Journal for January 20, 2015 at 09:08PM
item.description: Progress on the walkway

item.title: Photo Journal for January 19, 2015 at 05:23PM
item.description: Sometimes life is full of sh!*. RIP big man.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 18, 2015 at 03:20PM
item.description: There was one important thing missing from today’s festivities. Get better soon sweetie pie.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 17, 2015 at 07:56PM
item.description: Birthday breakfast for Mary at Soup Spoon

item.title: Photo Journal for January 16, 2015 at 07:37PM
item.description: After a week on call this is necessary

item.title: Photo Journal for January 15, 2015 at 11:28AM
item.description: It’s official! Sworn in today. Please let me know how I can help you or those you know with your real estate needs. I would forever be grateful and promise to do all that I can to make your experience as enjoyable as possible.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 14, 2015 at 08:45AM
item.description: OMFG and it was reading -17 at one point

item.title: Photo Journal for January 13, 2015 at 09:37PM
item.description: Thankful that my wife has introduced me to so many new foods, but also that my parents didn’t force things on me – making me more open to learn new things at my own pace.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 12, 2015 at 08:43PM
item.description: Another one bites the dust

item.title: Photo Journal for January 10, 2015 at 10:37PM
item.description: Mary’s Ladies Breakfast Run was a huge success even w the temp in single digits.

item.title: Photo Journal for January 11, 2015 at 10:39PM
item.description: It’s the ties vs the triangles at Packers Stadium (yes, that is the concession stand in the background)

item.title: Photo Journal for January 09, 2015 at 05:38PM
item.description: First “snow day” of the year. Frickin freezing!

item.title: Photo Journal for January 08, 2015 at 10:05PM
item.description: If I’ve had to stare at it all day then so do you 🙂

item.title: Photo Journal for January 07, 2015 at 06:37PM
item.description: Possibly the single most meaningful, amazing, and beautiful gift I’ve ever received. Thank you so much Uncle Randy!

item.title: Photo Journal for January 06, 2015 at 10:10PM
item.description:
Playing the I hate cars game

For any of my friends who enjoy a car mystery… This is the 2006 Honda Odyssey. Yesterday around 1pm Mary got rear-ended but all seemed to be ok. It’s also colder than sin.

Today, after having kids go in and out of various doors without indecent she went to pick Al up from school. After getting in the van and putting it into reverse or drive the door ajar alarm (constant buzz) would sound until the car was put in park or neutral. It did this until she got home (drove home with the buzz) and left it for me.

When I went to look at it I noticed the same behavior and also noted that none of the door ajar dashboard lights were on. After fiddling with some things (mostly trying to diagnose a pattern), I discovered that if you put the rear doors into manual mode via the driver side dashboard switch the sound went away. Score 1 for the good guys.

Unfortunately, I knew someone wouldn’t consider that a permanent fix so I went looking for another solution. What I found online suggested removing the drivers side #7 fuse labeled “back up”, so I gave that a shot. When you remove that fuse the beeping for doors open/key in the ignition stops and the rear power doors do not work at all. After waiting the prescribed 30-60 seconds I replaced the fuse and the constant buzz in drive/reverse is no longer there, but now the passenger side sliding door does not open automatically (just manually).

So I and apparently Google appear to be out of ideas. That and I’m tired. If any of you can help me w/ a solution that doesn’t require a trip to the mechanic there’s many beers or other beverages of choice in your future.
